news.title:
KEC Intl, HBL Engineering, and Kernex Microsystems gain focus on Kavach updates.
HBL secured ₹1,375.4Cr orders; Kernex partners with BHEL for advanced signalling solutions.
news.summary:
KEC Intl, HBL Engineering, and Kernex Microsystems gain focus on Kavach updates.
HBL secured ₹1,375.4Cr orders; Kernex partners with BHEL for advanced signalling solutions.

news.title:
HBL Engineering Ltd secures Rs.179.79 Cr order from Banaras Locomotive Works for KAVACH equipment.
Contract includes supply, testing, commissioning; to be completed by Feb 15, 2027. Promoters not involved.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering Ltd secures Rs.179.79 Cr order from Banaras Locomotive Works for KAVACH equipment.
Contract includes supply, testing, commissioning; to be completed by Feb 15, 2027. Promoters not involved.

news.title:
Analysts recommend Buy on L&T at Rs 3,588 and RIL near Rs 1,270-1,300; Hold for Canara Bank, MRPL, Piramal Pharma.
Avoid Shakti Pumps; Tata Motors, Vimta Labs advised as Wait and Watch; HBL Engineering Buy on declines; ITC Sell on rise.
news.summary:
Analysts recommend Buy on L&T at Rs 3,588 and RIL near Rs 1,270-1,300; Hold for Canara Bank, MRPL, Piramal Pharma.
Avoid Shakti Pumps; Tata Motors, Vimta Labs advised as Wait and Watch; HBL Engineering Buy on declines; ITC Sell on rise.

news.title:
HBL Engineering Ltd forms JV with Cochin Shipyard to develop electric mobility tech and energy storage for maritime sector.
The JV, named Green Maritime Propulsion Pvt Ltd, will have HBL holding 60% and Cochin Shipyard 40%, with a capital of Rs. 9 crore.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering Ltd forms JV with Cochin Shipyard to develop electric mobility tech and energy storage for maritime sector.
The JV, named Green Maritime Propulsion Pvt Ltd, will have HBL holding 60% and Cochin Shipyard 40%, with a capital of Rs. 9 crore.

news.title:
CARE Ratings reaffirmed CARE A (Positive) and CARE A1 for HBL Engineering Ltd, citing FY25 and 9MFY26 performance.
Working capital limits rating withdrawn as the company surrendered the limits.
news.summary:
CARE Ratings reaffirmed CARE A (Positive) and CARE A1 for HBL Engineering Ltd, citing FY25 and 9MFY26 performance.
Working capital limits rating withdrawn as the company surrendered the limits.

news.title:
HBL Engineering Ltd secures ₹800.4 cr contract from Banaras Locomotive Works for Kavach equipment.
The project involves on-board Kavach equipment supply, to be completed within 12 months.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering Ltd secures ₹800.4 cr contract from Banaras Locomotive Works for Kavach equipment.
The project involves on-board Kavach equipment supply, to be completed within 12 months.

news.title:
HBL Engineering Ltd approved unaudited Q3 FY26 financials with consolidated income at Rs.884.6 cr, net profit Rs.217.7 cr.
Declared interim dividend of Rs.2/share; approved joint venture with Cochin Shipyard and equity investments in startups.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering Ltd approved unaudited Q3 FY26 financials with consolidated income at Rs.884.6 cr, net profit Rs.217.7 cr.
Declared interim dividend of Rs.2/share; approved joint venture with Cochin Shipyard and equity investments in startups.

news.title:
Railway stocks likely to gain from 15% rise in FY26 railway capex, focusing on execution, safety upgrades, and capacity expansion.
RITES, Titagarh Rail Systems, CG Power, HBL Power, and Kernex Microsystems seen as beneficiaries of modernization and infrastructure thrust.
news.summary:
Railway stocks likely to gain from 15% rise in FY26 railway capex, focusing on execution, safety upgrades, and capacity expansion.
RITES, Titagarh Rail Systems, CG Power, HBL Power, and Kernex Microsystems seen as beneficiaries of modernization and infrastructure thrust.

news.title:
HBL Engineering Ltd secures a Rs.575 Cr order from Integral Coach Factory, Chennai.
The order includes supply, testing, commissioning of KAVACH Ver.4.0, to be completed in 12 months.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering Ltd secures a Rs.575 Cr order from Integral Coach Factory, Chennai.
The order includes supply, testing, commissioning of KAVACH Ver.4.0, to be completed in 12 months.

news.title:
Cochin Shipyard proposes JV with HBL Engineering for electric marine tech.
JV shareholding: HBL 60%, CSL 40%. Focus on electric mobility, energy storage.
news.summary:
Cochin Shipyard proposes JV with HBL Engineering for electric marine tech.
JV shareholding: HBL 60%, CSL 40%. Focus on electric mobility, energy storage.

news.title:
HBL Engineering Ltd's board will meet on Feb 7, 2026, to review Q3 FY26 results and consider an interim dividend.
Trading window reopens 48 hours post-meeting as per SEBI regulations and insider trading code.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering Ltd's board will meet on Feb 7, 2026, to review Q3 FY26 results and consider an interim dividend.
Trading window reopens 48 hours post-meeting as per SEBI regulations and insider trading code.

news.title: HBL Engineering shares dropped 13%, reasons for this decline explained.
news.summary: HBL Engineering shares dropped 13%, reasons for this decline explained.

news.title:
HBL Engineering lost the CLW loco Kavach tender for 6,300 units due to higher pricing.
The firm expects Rs.1000 cr from loco units and Rs.900 cr from Kavach orders in FY27.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering lost the CLW loco Kavach tender for 6,300 units due to higher pricing.
The firm expects Rs.1000 cr from loco units and Rs.900 cr from Kavach orders in FY27.

news.title:
HBL Engineering delivered 1,659 of 2,200 ordered units by Dec 2025 deadline.
2024 tender totals 10,000 units; only 3,000 delivered across suppliers so far.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering delivered 1,659 of 2,200 ordered units by Dec 2025 deadline.
2024 tender totals 10,000 units; only 3,000 delivered across suppliers so far.

news.title:
HBL Engineering shipped 1,659 of 2,200 Loco TCAS units by Dec 13, 2025.
541 units were canceled; next year's demand is projected at 18,429 units.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering shipped 1,659 of 2,200 Loco TCAS units by Dec 13, 2025.
541 units were canceled; next year's demand is projected at 18,429 units.

news.title:
HBL Engineering posts Q2 net profit of ₹3.87b, up from ₹872.6m YoY.
Revenue surged to ₹12.22b; EBITDA margin improved to 44.5% from 20.8% YoY.
news.summary:
HBL Engineering posts Q2 net profit of ₹3.87b, up from ₹872.6m YoY.
Revenue surged to ₹12.22b; EBITDA margin improved to 44.5% from 20.8% YoY.
